aliases: Sri Lankan Civil War final conventional phase
family_summary: Final conventional phase of the Sri Lankan civil war, beginning with the resumption of major hostilities after the Mavil Aru crisis in July 2006 and ending with the military destruction of the LTTE in May 2009. The war moved through distinct eastern, northern, and final-pocket phases with sharply changing civilian exposure and battlefield geometry.
notes: Episode-first bundle emphasizing displacement-sensitive person-month denominators. Only the best-sourced final-phase sub-episode is fully estimated; later final-pocket episodes are proposed but not fully quantified because source separation between civilian and LTTE casualties degrades.
